key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 operating cash flow $118.3M less capital spending $11.3M ÷ 31.668M diluted shares per ACLS/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260226_rpt20251231_acc0001104659_26_020461.htm
Mid-cycle normalization on Purion ion implant franchise; services mix stable

open_questions: [ ] Confirm mid-cycle normalization ($3.81/sh) vs spot $3.38/sh before stance promotion
[ ] Backlog trend: two consecutive quarter-ends below $400M would widen cycle reserve
[ ] No approved third-party sources in base IRR; re-scan when sell-side coverage indexed
thesis: Axcelis Technologies (NASDAQ: ACLS) designs, manufactures, and services ion implantation equipment used in semiconductor fabrication. **[Fact]** The Purion product family accounts for 98.2% of revenue; the remaining 1.8% is aftermarket revenue on legacy systems. **[Fact]** FY2025 consolidated revenue was **$839 million** (systems **$571 million**, services **$268 million**), down from **$1,018 million** in 2024 and a **$1,131 million** peak in 2023. **[Fact]** The company carries **no term debt**, **$146 million** in cash, and **$457 million** of systems backlog including deferred systems revenue at December 31, 2025. **[Inference]** Owner cash today is positive but cyclically depressed; the investment case rests on Purion share in power-device and mature-node implants plus backlog conversion as wafer-fabrication equipment spending recovers.
why_market_wrong: The market may be pricing **SiC, power-device, and memory implant recovery** while underweighting **backlog erosion** (systems backlog fell from **$646 million** to **$457 million** year over year), **international concentration** (84% of revenue outside the United States, with heavy Asia exposure), and **trough economics** (FY2025 free cash flow **$3.38 per share** on **$131.70** price implies a rich multiple on spot cash generation). **[Inference]** Q1 2026 revenue of roughly **$199 million** is stable versus the prior year but still far below 2024 run rates; normalized mid-cycle owner cash near **$3.81 per share** supports a partial dhando floor, not a high-teens annual return at today's price.
